steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank is part of a compound predicate with 'had spent.' The verb must agree with the singular subject 'the song' and stay in past tense to match the surrounding context.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'have been ranking': Present perfect tense conflicts with the past-tense context established by 'had spent.' ✗
  • Choice B — 'was ranked': Singular ('was' agrees with 'the song') and past tense (matching 'had spent'). ✓
  • Choice C — 'were ranked': Past tense is correct, but 'were' is plural and does not agree with the singular subject 'the song.' ✗
  • Choice D — 'are ranking': Present tense conflicts with the past-tense context. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
